parameters.name: red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: Select which method is used to calculate the slope of the Red channel. Horizontal, Vertical, and Z options let you calculate the slope by sampling points horizontally, vertically, or in the z directio

parameters.name: green
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description: Select which method is used to calulate the slope of the Green channel. Horizontal, Vertical, and Z options let you calculate the slope by sampling points horizontally, vertically, or in the z directi

parameters.name: blue
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description: Select which method is used to calulate the slope of the Blue channel. Horizontal, Vertical, and Z options let you calculate the slope by sampling points horizontally, vertically, or in the z directio

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description: Select which method is used to calculate the slope of the Alpha channel. Horizontal, Vertical, and Z options let you calculate the slope by sampling points horizontally, vertically, or in the z direct

parameters.name: method
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: Determines what pixels to use when calculating the slope at each pixel in the image.

parameters.name: zeropoint
parameters.label: Zero Point
parameters.description: Sets the value to output when the slope is zero, similar to a midpoint. Default is .5 since 8-bit pxels are 0 to 1. But with Pixel Format et to 32-bit Float you should set this to 0, and look at the v

parameters.name: strength
parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description: Set the strength of the output using this multiplier. Higher values result in higher slope values.

parameters.name: offsetunit
parameters.label: Sample Step Unit
parameters.description: Sets the units used in the Sample Step parameter.

parameters.name: extend
parameters.label: Extend
parameters.description: Sets the extend conditions to determine what happens to the slope at the edge of the image.

parameters.name: offset3
parameters.label: Offset3
parameters.description: When sampling the image, this determines the distance from each pixel to the sample pixel. When units are set to pixels, it is the number of pixels away from the current pixel which is sampled to find

examples.label: Configure Green and Blue
examples.description: Common settings from 5 real Derivative examples
